author: thegaram33
content:
Hey all, I've been building a prototype of Block-in-Blobs (EIP-8142) on ethrex and Lighthouse. We now have a local Kurtosis devnet running BiB end-to-end, plus integration into the EIP-8025 guest program.

Main learnings so far:
1. Moving KZG (or whatever pq-DA scheme replaces it) onto the Engine API adds latency, a potential liveness risk.
2. BiB includes the BAL which cannot be serialized incrementally (it's only fully known once the block is built). This complicates payload building and blob-count accounting.
3. How payload blobs should interact with the blob fee market is still an open research question.

Full writeup: https://hackmd.io/@peter-scroll/eip-8142-block-in-blobs. I'd love any questions or feedback, here or on HackMD.
